intro.title: Repair & Reconnect
intro.description: Conflict can create painful distance between partners. Rebuilding trust and safety doesn't require fixing everything at once — just one intentional step toward repair.
intro.quote: "Repair is the lifeline of a relationship. It's not about never fighting; it's about how you reconnect after."
intro.small_efforts: Small, vulnerable efforts create the foundation for emotional safety.

choose_person.title: Who would you like to repair with?
choose_person.desc: Focusing on a specific relationship helps ground your steps in real connection.

choose_approach.title: What approach feels right for you?
choose_approach.desc: Select one gentle action step to begin mending the bond.
choose_approach.safety: You don't need to resolve the whole conflict today — focus on one doable step.

other.label: Reach out with a simple note
Let go of the defensive stance

guided_action.safety_line_default: There is no pressure to send anything. You are writing for your own clarity. 💛
guided_action.safety_line_pause: Take a breath — pace yourself. Reconnection takes time. 💛
guided_action.safety_line_reflect: This reflection is completely private, for your own growth. 💛

message.title: Send a vulnerable check-in
message.why: Vulnerable reaching out invites safety, stepping away from defensive posture and showing care.
message.insight: Sharing your own longing or softness is often the quickest way to de-escalate tension.
message.howBody: Avoid explaining your side or bringing up past mistakes. Share a simple feeling of caring or regret.
prompts: I miss you and hate the distance between us. Can we talk when you're ready?
I know things got tense. I care about you more than winning the argument.
I'm sorry for my part in our conflict. I want to listen and hear how you're feeling.

acknowledge.title: Take accountability
acknowledge.why: Owning your contribution to the cycle shows maturity and makes your partner feel safe.
acknowledge.insight: Acknowledging your part allows your partner to drop their shield and listen.
acknowledge.howBody: Name what you did and how it might have impacted them. Avoid using 'but' or mentioning what they did.
prompts: I realize I reacted defensively and shut down. I'm sorry for closing off.
I notice I raised my voice and spoke critically. I want to handle our disagreements with more care.
I was focusing so much on my point that I stopped listening to you. I'm sorry.

pause.title: Propose a calm pause
pause.why: A structured pause prevents reactive escalations and protects both partners from saying hurtful things.
pause.insight: Stepping away only works if you reassure your partner that you will return to finish the conversation.
pause.howBody: Clearly state that you feel overwhelmed and need time to calm down, but explicitly promise to check back in.
prompts: I'm feeling too flooded to hear you clearly right now. Let's take a 20-minute break, and I'll check back in.
I want to listen to you fully, but I need a moment to regulate. Let's pause and talk after dinner.
I can feel myself getting defensive. Let's take a pause so I can calm my nervous system before we continue.

letgo.title: Initiate Reconnection
letgo.why: Actions and presence often speak louder than words in healing attachment wounds.
letgo.insight: Reconnecting can be non-verbal — a gentle touch, a hug, or just making coffee can signal safety.
letgo.howBody: Choose a small, low-demand physical action or gesture that shows you want to connect, not fight.
prompts: I want to sit next to you quietly, without talk of the argument, just to be close.
I'm offering a gentle hug or hand-hold to remind us that we are on the same team.
I'm going to make us a warm drink as a quiet gesture of care.

reflect.title: Reflect on attachment fears
reflect.why: Underneath relationship anger is almost always a fear of rejection, abandonment, or inadequacy.
reflect.insight: Understanding your own triggers helps you speak from vulnerability instead of anger.
reflect.howBody: Reflect on what the argument made you feel about yourself or the relationship. Was it fear of losing them, or feeling not good enough?
prompts: When my partner pulled away, I felt panicked and feared they didn't care about me anymore.
When my partner criticized me, I felt inadequate and like I can never do anything right.
I realized my reaction was driven by a deep longing to feel secure and valued by my partner.

complete.title: Repair Attempt Made
complete.message: You've taken a brave step toward emotional repair. Reconnection grows through honesty, care, and mutual accountability.

common.completion_message: You've successfully completed this activity. Take a moment to appreciate your progress.
